title: 10 best herbal tinctures for eye strain
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for eye strain are concentrated liquid extracts made from plants and herbs that have natural anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and soothing properties. They help alleviate eye strain by reducing redness, itching, and fatigue associated with prolonged screen time, reading, or other visual activities. Examples of herbal tinctures that show promise in combating eye strain include Ginkgo biloba, which improves blood flow to the eyes, and Eyebright, which reduces inflammation and irritation. By using these tinctures, individuals can improve their overall eye health, reduce eye fatigue, and enjoy improved focus and vision throughout the day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tinctures helps with eye strain because it contains a unique combination of flavonoids, antioxidants, and terpenes that work together to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes. The tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe and calm irritated eyes, while its antioxidant activity helps to protect against damage caused by blue light exposure and digital fatigue. This natural remedy can provide relief from eye strain and promote overall eye health.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Ginkgo biloba tincture contains potent antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, which can contribute to eye strain.

Vasodilation properties: The flavonoids present in Ginkgo biloba tincture can help dilate blood vessels, improving blood flow and oxygenation to the eyes, which can alleviate eye fatigue and discomfort.

Neuroprotective effects: Ginkgo biloba has been shown to have neuroprotective effects on the optic nerve, potentially helping to reduce the risk of age-related macular degeneration (AMD) and other eye diseases that may contribute to eye strain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the maidenhair tree are rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing swelling and discomfort associated with eye strain.
Barks: The barks of the maidenhair tree contain soothing agents that help calm and relax the eyes, alleviating tension and stress caused by prolonged screen time.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality ginkgo biloba leaves in the fall season when they are at their peak potency.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator or air drying method for 7-10 days.
Chop the dried ginkgo biloba leaves into small pieces and combine with 100-proof vodka to create a 1:2 ratio mixture.
Steep the leaf-vodka mixture in a glass jar for 2 weeks, shaking daily, before straining and filtering the liquid.
Transfer the filtered tincture to dark glass bottles, store in a cool dry place, and use within 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Blueberry tinctures helps with eye strain because of its unique combination of antioxidants and flavonoids. These compounds work together to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, which can contribute to fatigue, dryness, and discomfort caused by prolonged screen time or other visual activities. The tincture's anti-inflammatory properties also help to soothe and calm the eyes, providing relief from irritation and redness. By supporting eye health, blueberry tinctures can help to alleviate eye strain and promote overall visual well-being.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Blueberries contain powerful antioxidants, such as anthocyanins and phenolic acids, which help to neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress in the eyes, thereby alleviating eye strain caused by digital device use.

Anti-Inflammatory Effects: The bioactive compounds present in blueberry tinctures have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes, relieving discomfort and pain associated with eye strain.

Vasoprotective Properties: Blueberries contain flavonoids that can help to improve blood flow to the eyes and protect against vascular damage, which is essential for maintaining healthy vision and reducing symptoms of eye strain such as blurred vision and dryness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, blueberry roots help soothe and protect the eyes from strain caused by digital fatigue.
Barks: Containing astringent and anti-inflammatory compounds, blueberry barks reduce redness and puffiness associated with eye strain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality vaccinium myrtillus berries and clean them thoroughly removing any stems or debris.
Combine 200 grams of the cleaned berries with 400 milliliters of 95% ethanol in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 14 days at room temperature allowing the solvents to extract active compounds effectively.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and discard the solids filtering only the clear liquid remaining.
Transfer the resulting tincture to dark glass bottles and store it in the refrigerator at a consistent temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eyebright tinctures helps with eye strain because it possesses potent anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated eyes. The herbal extract reduces redness, itchiness, and fatigue caused by prolonged screen time, reading, or other visual activities. Eyebright's antimicrobial agents also help to remove impurities and toxins from the eyes, promoting healthy vision and reducing the risk of infections. Additionally, eyebright tinctures can help to improve tear quality and reduce dryness, making it an effective natural remedy for alleviating eye strain and promoting overall ocular health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Eyebright's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling and discomfort caused by eye strain, making it a natural remedy for relieving red, irritated eyes.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in eyebright tincture help protect the eyes from free radical damage, which can contribute to age-related macular degeneration, cataracts, and other eye problems exacerbated by prolonged screen time or eye strain.

Vasoconstrictive properties: Eyebright's vasoconstrictive compounds help reduce blood vessel dilation in the eyes, which can alleviate symptoms of conjunctivitis, pink eye, and other conditions characterized by excessive tearing, redness, and discharge.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Eyebright contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Buds: The buds of Eyebright are rich in saponins, which help to soothe and calm irritated eyes, reducing redness and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried euphrasia officinalis leaves and flowers at dawn or before sunrise for optimal potency.
Combine one part of the euphrasia material with two parts of vodka or 95% grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for eight to ten weeks, shaking it daily, to allow for maceration and extraction.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.
Label and store the finished euphrasia officinalis tincture in a dark glass bottle at room temperature for up to two years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Grape tinctures helps with eye strain because it contains a blend of potent herbs that work synergistically to reduce fatigue, improve circulation, and relax ocular muscles. The anti-inflammatory properties of grape tincture help to alleviate dryness and irritation caused by prolonged screen time or reading, while its antioxidant-rich compounds neutralize free radicals that can damage the eyes. By soothing and nourishing the eyes, herbal grape tinctures provide natural relief from eye strain, promoting comfortable vision and a sense of clarity and focus.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Grape tincture's antioxidant properties help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, which can contribute to eye strain and fatigue.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in grape tincture, such as resveratrol and quercetin, can help to reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with eye strain.

Vasodilatory effects: Grape tincture's vasodilatory properties can help to dilate blood vessels in the eyes, improving blood flow and reducing pressure on the eyes, which can contribute to eye strain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Grape roots have been traditionally used in herbal remedies to reduce inflammation and improve circulation, making them suitable for treating eye strain caused by fatigue and stress.
Barks: The barks of certain grape species contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that can help soothe and protect the eyes from strain and irritation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh grape leaves from the vitis vinifera plant in early summer morning ensuring optimal potency and flavor.
Clean and dry the harvested grape leaves by gently brushing away dirt particles using a soft-bristled brush.
Combine one part dried grape leaves with one part vodka or high-proof alcohol in a clean glass jar sealing it tightly for maceration.
Allow the mixture to steep for 14 days at room temperature away from direct sunlight and moisture ensuring optimal extraction.

Strain the tincture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into a clean dark glass bottle discarding solids and labeling contents accurately with ingredients dosages preparation time and expiration date.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric tinctures helps with eye strain because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and irritation in the eyes. The bioactive compound curcumin, found in turmeric, has been shown to alleviate dryness, itchiness, and blurred vision caused by extended screen time or other environmental factors. Additionally, turmeric's antioxidant properties help protect the delicate eye tissues from oxidative stress, promoting overall ocular health and reducing the likelihood of chronic eye problems. As a result, herbal turmeric tinctures provide a natural and effective solution for relieving eye strain and promoting healthy vision.
remedy_properties: Curcuminoids: These compounds have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, alleviating symptoms of eye strain such as dryness, redness, and blurred vision.

Antioxidant activity: Turmeric's antioxidant properties can protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to age-related macular degeneration and other eye problems, reducing the risk of chronic eye strain and promoting overall eye health.

Anti-inflammatory cytokine regulation: Curcumin in turmeric tinctures has been shown to modulate pro-inflammatory cytokines, reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy balance of immune responses in the eyes, which can help alleviate symptoms of eye strain such as redness, itching, and sensitivity to light.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes are a rich source of curcumin, which has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help soothe and protect the eyes from strain.
Roots: Turmeric roots contain a compound called turmerin, which has been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, making it effective for eye strain relief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried turmeric root weighing about 100 grams.
Grind the turmeric root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar.
Combine 10 parts of the turmeric powder with 90 parts of vodka or grain alcohol in a glass jar.
Allow the mixture to macerate for at least 14 days and up to 28 days in a cool dark place.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Saffron tinctures helps with eye strain because it contains a unique combination of antioxidants, anti-inflammatory compounds, and carotenoids that work together to soothe and relax the eyes. The bioactive compound crocin in saffron has been shown to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the retina, which can contribute to eye strain and fatigue caused by prolonged screen time or reading. Regular use of herbal saffron tinctures may help alleviate symptoms such as dryness, itchiness, and blurred vision associated with eye strain.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Saffron's antioxidants help neutralize free radicals that can damage the eyes and contribute to eye strain, reducing inflammation and promoting overall eye health.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Saffron's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling and irritation in the eyes, alleviating symptoms of eye strain such as dryness, redness, and itchiness.

Vasodilatory properties: Saffron's vasodilating effects help increase blood flow to the eyes, improving oxygenation and nutrient delivery to the delicate tissues and reducing fatigue and discomfort associated with eye strain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Saffron roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm irritated eyes, making them an effective ingredient in medicinal tinctures for eye strain.
Barks: The barks of the saffron plant contain compounds that have been shown to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, making it a valuable addition to eye strain remedies.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried crocus sativus flowers in the morning after sunrise when their petals are fully opened.
Weigh out 100g of dried flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Combine the ground flowers with 500ml of high-proof vodka in a glass jar, leaving one inch of space at the top, and store it in a cool dark place for 2 weeks.
Shake the jar twice daily for 10 seconds each time to extract the bioactive compounds from the crocus sativus into the vodka for 14 days.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids, and store it in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian tinctures helps with eye strain because they promote relaxation and reduce inflammation in the eyes. The soothing properties of valerian root calm irritated eyes, while its antioxidant compounds help to combat oxidative stress that can contribute to fatigue and discomfort. Additionally, valerian's ability to regulate sleep patterns ensures that you're well-rested and less prone to digital eye strain caused by prolonged screen time. By reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, herbal valerian tinctures provide natural relief from eye strain.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Valerian root's antioxidant compounds help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress that can contribute to eye strain.
Calcium channel blocking activity: Valerian root's calcium channel blockers help relax muscles in the eye and surrounding areas, reducing spasms and tension that can cause eye strain and fatigue.

Sedative and anxiolytic properties: Valerian root's sedative and anxiolytic compounds help calm the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety that can exacerbate eye strain and promote relaxation of the eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress that contribute to eye strain.
Buds: Contain volatile oils that have a calming effect on the eyes and can help alleviate dryness and irritation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried valeriana officinalis root and separate it from any excess plant material.
Chop the valeriana officinalis root into small pieces and pack it tightly into a clean glass jar.
Pour 80 proof vodka over the chopped herb, ensuring it completely covers all the root material.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 6 weeks, shaking occasionally to combine the mixture.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle and discard solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile tinctures helps with eye strain because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. When applied topically to the eyes, chamomile tincture can help reduce redness, itching, and swelling caused by prolonged screen time or fatigue. The herb's soothing effects can also calm dry, irritated eyes and improve overall visual comfort. Additionally, chamomile's gentle astringent properties can help constrict blood vessels in the eyes, reducing puffiness and dark circles. As a result, regular use of herbal chamomile tinctures can provide long-lasting relief from eye strain and promote healthy, comfortable vision.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce redness and swelling in the eyes, providing relief from eye strain caused by prolonged screen time or other visual stimuli.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in chamomile tincture neutralize free radicals that can damage ocular tissues, reducing the risk of long-term eye damage and promoting overall eye health.

Anxiolytic (calming) properties: Chamomile's calming effects help reduce stress and anxiety, which are common contributors to eye strain and tension headaches; by relaxing the muscles around the eyes, chamomile tincture can alleviate discomfort and promote relaxation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the chamomile plant contain a high concentration of apigenin, an antioxidant that helps to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation, making them effective in treating eye strain.
Flowers: Chamomile flowers are rich in azulene, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm irritated eyes and relieve symptoms of eye strain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest matricaria chamomilla flowers in their blooming stage at dawn when essential oils are most concentrated.
Dry the harvested flowers using a food dehydrator on low heat for 6-8 hours to preserve potency.
Combine 10 parts dried matricaria chamomilla flowers with 80% ethanol and allow it to steep for 4 weeks in a glass jar.
Shake the jar daily during the steeping process to enhance extraction of active compounds from the plant material.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and labeling the preparation as ready.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with eye strain because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness and swelling in the eyes. The glycyrrhizin present in licorice root has been shown to have antioxidant effects, which can protect the eyes from damage caused by blue light and other environmental stressors. Additionally, licorice tinctures can help relax the muscles around the eyes, reducing tension and fatigue that can contribute to eye strain. By addressing these underlying causes, herbal licorice tinctures can provide natural relief for individuals experiencing eye strain.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce redness and swelling associated with eye strain, promoting healthy vision and reducing discomfort.

Licoricidin: This flavonoid has antioxidant properties, which can help neutralize free radicals that may contribute to eye fatigue and dryness, protecting the eyes from damage caused by prolonged screen time or other forms of eye strain.

Glycyrrhetic acid: This compound has a natural soothing effect on mucous membranes, including those in the eyes, which can help calm irritated eyes and reduce itchiness and burning sensations associated with eye strain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps soothe and calm irritated eyes, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
Barks: Containing anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, the barks help protect the eyes from further damage and promote recovery from strain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root and crush it into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered root with 150 milliliters of 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar, ensuring good contact between the two ingredients.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days, shaking the jar twice daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into another container, squeezing as much liquid from the solids as possible and discarding the residue.
Transfer the resulting tincture to amber glass bottles, storing them in a refrigerator at 2-8°c for up to two years or until fully consumed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hawthorn tinctures helps with eye strain because of its unique combination of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and anthocyanins. These potent antioxidants help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, which can contribute to digital eye fatigue. By soothing and protecting the delicate tissues surrounding the eyes, hawthorn tinctures promote healthy vision and alleviate discomfort associated with prolonged screen use.
remedy_properties: Vasodilatory effects: Hawthorn's vasodilatory properties help to relax and dilate blood vessels, which can reduce eye pressure and alleviate symptoms of eye strain such as headaches and blurred vision.

Antioxidant activity: Hawthorn's antioxidant compounds help to neutralize free radicals that can damage the eyes and contribute to eye strain, promoting overall eye health and reducing the risk of age-related macular degeneration.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Hawthorn's anti-inflammatory compounds can reduce inflammation in the eyes, which can help alleviate symptoms of eye strain such as redness, itchiness, and dryness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Hawthorn roots have been traditionally used to reduce inflammation and improve circulation, making them effective in alleviating eye strain caused by increased blood pressure.
Barks: The barks of the hawthorn tree contain flavonoids that can help constrict blood vessels and reduce swelling, providing relief from eye strain and fatigue.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh c. monogyna leaves at dawn in summer and dry them thoroughly for 2-3 days.
Combine 250g of dried c. monogyna leaves with 750ml of 45% vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a dark cupboard for 6 weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth into another container, discarding the solids and measuring the final volume.
Transfer the filtered tincture to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids, labeling and storing them in a cool place for future use.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat eye strain with herbal tinctures is to combine a few select botanicals. Ginkgo biloba and Bilberry extract help reduce fatigue and improve vision clarity. Peppermint oil calms the eyes and reduces dryness, while Eyebright tincture soothes inflammation and promotes healing. Mix equal parts of each tincture in water or eye wash solution and apply gently to the affected area to alleviate discomfort and promote recovery.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to eye strain that are treated with herbal tinctures are those affecting the visual system, nervous system, and overall well-being. Herbal tinctures such as Ginkgo biloba, Bacopa monnieri, and Ashwagandha help alleviate symptoms of fatigue, headaches, and dry eyes associated with conditions like computer vision syndrome, nearsightedness, and presbyopia. Additionally, adaptogenic herbs like Rhodiola rosea and Schisandra chinensis support stress resilience and mental clarity, further contributing to overall eye health and visual comfort.
